content.problemStatement:
You are given the heights of the students of a class consisting of nnn students. Your task is to find the maximum length of an increasing subarray of the given array.

A subarray is the sequence of consecutive elements of the array. Subarray is called increasing if each element of this subarray is strictly greater than the previous.


Input Format
The first line contains a single positive integer nnn — the number of students.


The second line contains nnn positive integers a1,a2,...,ana1,a2,...,ana1,a2,...,an - the heights of students.



Output Format
Print the maximum length of an increasing subarray of the given array.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 10), the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 100000) – the number of students. The second line contains n positive integers a1, a2, ..., an (1 ≤ ai ≤ 10^9) – the heights of the students.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer on its own line – the maximum length of a strictly increasing subarray of the given array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: All heights are equal, so the longest strictly increasing subarray consists of any single element, length 1.
The whole array is strictly increasing, giving length 3.
The longest increasing subarrays are [1,2,3] and [2,4,5], each of length 3.

solution.approach: Traverse the array once while keeping two counters: 'cur' – length of the current strictly increasing subarray ending at the current position, and 'ans' – the maximum length seen so far. Initialise both to 1 (a single element is always an increasing subarray). For each element from the second onward, if it is greater than the previous element, increment 'cur'; otherwise reset 'cur' to 1. After each step update 'ans' with max(ans, cur). This yields the answer in O(n) time and O(1) extra space per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, where n is the size of the array. Overall O( Σ n ) for all test cases.
